abstract: ABSTRACT Although Assessment Center (AC) role‐play assessments have received ample attention in past research, their reliance on actual behavioral information is still unclear. Uncovering the behavioral basis of AC role‐play assessments is, however, a prerequisite for the optimization of existing and the development of novel automated AC procedures. This work provides a first data‐driven benchmark for the behavioral prediction and explanation of AC performance judgments. We used machine learning models trained on behavioral cues ( C = 36) to predict performance judgments in three interpersonal AC exercises from a real‐life high‐stakes AC (selection of medical students, N = 199). Three main findings emerged: First, behavioral prediction models showed substantial predictive performance and outperformed prediction models representing potential judgment biases. Comparisons with in‐sample results revealed overfitting of traditional approaches, highlighting the importance of out‐of‐sample evaluations. Second, we demonstrate that linear combinations of behavioral cues can be strong predictors of assessors' judgments. Third, we identified consistent exercise‐specific patterns of individual cues and cross‐exercise consistent behavioral patterns of behavioral dimensions and interpersonal strategies that were especially predictive of the assessors' judgments. We discuss implications for future research and practice.

ob.ai_reason: 评估中心行为预测，机器学习为方法非核心
ob.tldr: 评估中心角色扮演评估的行为基础尚不清晰。研究以 199 名医学生选拔数据训练机器学习模型，基于 36 个行为线索预测人际演练中的绩效评判。结果显示行为预测模型具有实质性预测效力，优于传统偏差模型；线性行为线索组合是强预测因子，且存在跨演练一致的行为模式，为开发自动化评估中心程序提供数据基准。

title: Predicting and Explaining Assessment Center Judgments: A Cross‐Validated Behavioral Approach to Performance Judgments in Interpersonal Assessment Center Exercises
title_zh: 预测与解释评估中心判断：人际评估中心练习中绩效评判的交叉验证行为方法
